title: Elastic wave device
abstract: The present invention relates to an elastic wave device comprising a piezoelectric material (3) with first domains (3a1, 3a2) of a first polarization direction (13a) and second domains (3b1, 3b2) with a second polarization direction (13b), the first direction being opposite to the second direction, in which the first and second domains are periodically alternated along a direction, called periodic direction, perpendicular to the surface normal of the piezoelectric material, and a pair of interdigitated comb electrodes (15a, 15b), the respective comb teeth (17a1, 17a2, 17b1, 17b2) of which extend mainly in the periodic direction, and to a method for manufacturing such an elastic wave device.
claims:
Claims 1. Elastic wave device comprising a piezoelectric material, in particular a ferroelectric material (3), with first domains (3a) of a first polarization direction (13a) and second domains (3b) with a second polarization direction (13b), the first direction (13a) being opposite to the second direction, in which the first and second domains (3a, 3bi) are periodically alternated along a direction (d), called periodic direction, perpendicular to the surface normal (n) of the piezoelectric material (3), and a pair of interdigitated comb electrodes (15a, 15b), the respective comb teeth (17a1 , 17a2, 17b1 , 17b2) of which extend mainly along the periodic direction (d) or the comb teeth are deviated from the periodic direction by a maximum of Â±5Â°. 2. Elastic wave device according to claim 1, wherein the comb teeth are arranged periodically. 3. Elastic wave device according to claim 1 or 2, wherein the mechanical pitch of the electrodes (p e ) is greater than the pitch of the domains (pf). 4. Elastic wave device according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the interdigitated comb electrodes (15a, 15b) are arranged directly on or at least partially, preferably entirely, buried in the piezoelectric material (3). 5. Elastic wave device according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the first direction (13a) and the second direction (13b) are parallel to the surface normal (n) of the piezoelectric material (3). 6; Elastic wave device according to any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the width (a) of the teeth (17a1 , 17a2, 17b1 , 17b2) of the interdigitated comb electrodes (15a, 15b) is at least 280 nm, preferably at least 350 nm. 7. Elastic wave device according to any one of claims 1 to 6, comprising a supply means (21) configured to provide a radiofrequency signal of a frequency of at least 3 GHz, preferably at least 6 GHz, to the interdigitated comb electrodes (15a, 15b). 8. Elastic wave device according to any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the metallization ratio a/p e of the pair of interdigitated comb electrodes (15a, 15b) is between 0.3 and 0.75, preferably between 0.4 and 0.6, being specified that âaâ corresponds to the width of the teeth 

of the electrodes and âp e â to the pitch of the interdigitated comb electrodes (15a, 15b). 9. Elastic wave device according to one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the piezoelectric material (3) is arranged in the form of a layer (5), in particular with a thickness (e1) less than the wavelength, preferably less than A/2, and even more preferably less than A/4, above a base substrate (9), in particular a silicon, silicon oxide, silicon carbide (SiC), sapphire, silicon nitride (SiN), aluminium nitride (AIN), quartz, carbon, diamond, Yags, Yigs (Ytrium iron garnet) or LiNbCh under LiTaCh substrate. 10. Elastic wave device according to claim 9, wherein a dielectric layer (7), in particular a silicon oxide, SiN, Ta2Os, ZrC>2, HfC>2, SiON layer or a combination of these materials, is arranged between the piezoelectric material layer (5) and the base substrate (9), the dielectric layer (7) preferably having a thickness less than the wavelength and/or between 200 nm and 2 pm. 11. Elastic wave device according to any one of claims 9 or 10, wherein a layer, in particular a polycrystalline silicon or polycrystalline AIN or polycrystalline SiOCH layer, is arranged between the piezoelectric material (3) and the base substrate (9) or between the dielectric layer (7) and the base substrate (9), the trapping layer preferably having a thickness of between 300 nm and 2 pm. 12. Elastic wave device according to any one of claims 1 to 11 , wherein a metal layer is arranged on the surface of the piezoelectric material (3) on the opposite surface of the piezoelectric material the pair of interdigitated comb electrodes (15a, 15b), in particular between the piezoelectric material (3) layer (5) and the base substrate (9) or between the dielectric layer (3) and the base substrate (9). 13. Elastic wave device according to any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the piezoelectric material (3) is at least one from among LiTaCh, LiNbCh, KnbCh PZT, PMnPt, PbTiCh or AIScN. 14. Use of an elastic wave device according to claim 7, in an acousto-electric device, in particular a filter, a sensor or a delay line, having operating frequencies of 3 GHz and more, in particular, of 6 GHz or more. 15. Method for manufacturing an elastic wave device according to any one of claims 1 to 13 and comprising the following steps: 

- providing a piezoelectric material, in particular a ferroelectric material, - applying an electric field, stronger, in particular at least 10 times stronger, than the coercive field of the piezoelectric material to produce the first and second domains periodically alternating and having opposite polarizations, and - producing the pair of interdigitated comb electrodes on or at least partially, preferably fully, buried in the piezoelectric material. 16. Method for manufacturing an elastic wave device according to claim 15, wherein the electric field is applied in the form of parallel bands. 17. Method for manufacturing an elastic wave device according to claim 15 or 16, wherein the field lines are perpendicular to the surface of the piezoelectric material. 18. Method for manufacturing an elastic wave device according to at least one of claims 15 to 17, wherein the electric field is applied when the piezoelectric material has been heated to a temperature of at least 150Â°C, preferably to a temperature of at least 170Â°C.
